title: Classes and Objects 🏭
description: Learn to be the architect. Build blueprints from scratch.

slides.title: Ruby is Object-Oriented 💎
slides.content:
You've been using objects this whole time (Strings, Integers, Arrays). 

But the true power of Ruby lies in creating your very own Custom Objects.

To create a Custom Object, you must first design a **Class**.

slides.title: The Blueprint 📐
slides.content:
A Class is a blueprint. An Object is the actual house built from that blueprint.

You cannot live inside a blueprint. But once you have the blueprint, you can use it to build 100 actual, livable houses (objects).

```ruby
# Defining a Blueprint
class Dog
  # We will put code here soon
end
```

quizData.question: What is the relationship between a Class and an Object?

options.text: They are exactly the same thing.
A Class is the blueprint instruction manual. An Object is the actual item built from those instructions.
An Object is the blueprint. A Class is the item.

quizData.explanation: You write the `class` once to define the rules. Then you generate many Objects (also called 'Instances') from it.

slides.title: Class Naming Rules 🎩
slides.content:
Unlike variables and methods (which use `snake_case`), Class names MUST start with a Capital letter and use `CamelCase` without underscores.

Good: `class BankAccount`
Bad: `class bank_account`

slides.title: Instantiating an Object 🏭
slides.content:
To actually build an object from your blueprint, you attach the `.new` method to the Class name. 

Run this! See the weird computer output? That is the raw Object existing in memory!
slides.codeSnippet:
class Dog
end

# Creating an actual object from the blueprint
fido = Dog.new

puts fido

quizData.question: Which method officially generates a brand new Object using a Class blueprint?
quizData.explanation: Calling `.new` on any Class name (e.g., `Dog.new`) instantiates a fresh Object in your computer's RAM.
